---
title: "Day 2 — Variables, Expressions, and State"
subtitle: "Current value vs. planned value"
---

<!-- ============================================================
OUTLINE / SCAFFOLD. Generic examples only (current, planned, gap).
Core question: "What is the CURRENT value vs. the PLANNED value?"
============================================================ -->

## Current vs. planned state

<!-- TODO: a `planned_*` variable holds a proposal; the current/recorded
     variable only changes when an APPROVAL ASSIGNMENT copies planned -> current. -->

## Assignment does not auto-propagate

<!-- TODO: computing `planned = current + 2` leaves `current` unchanged
     until `current = planned` runs. This is the central Day 2 idea. -->

::: {.trap-band}
⚠️ **Watch out** — a planned value does **not** become current on its own. Nothing changes `current` until a line literally assigns to it.
:::

## Expressions & the gap pattern

<!-- TODO: derived values like `gap = target - current`.
     Trap: a gap can be computed from a PLANNED input while the recorded
     input is still old ("zero gap from planned, but current is stale"). -->

## Checkpoint language / state history

<!-- TODO: be able to say WHICH line made a value current —
     "after planning" vs. "after approval/recording". -->

::: {.check-band}
✅ **Non-mutating check** — `print(current)` or a bare `current` to see the recorded value without changing it. Never an assignment.
:::

## Worked trace

```{python}
#| eval: false
current = 8          # recorded value
planned = current + 2   # proposal only
# not approved yet
current              # still 8
current = planned    # approval assignment
current              # now 10
```

<!-- TODO: trace table. Columns: After line | current | planned | gap | reason -->

| After line | `current` | `planned` | reason |
|---|---|---|---|
| `current = 8` | 8 | — | baseline |
| `planned = current + 2` | 8 | 10 | proposal computed; current not reassigned |
| `current = planned` | 10 | 10 | approval makes planned current |

## Show your evidence

::: {.evidence-band}
🔎 **Show your evidence** — *trace* current vs. planned after each line; *debug* a log claim by naming whether a value is "after planning" or "after approval"; *explain* why checkpoint language matters.
:::
